paper: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
authors: Devlin, Jacob; Chang, Ming-Wei; Lee, Kenton; Toutanova, Kristina
summary: BERT was introduced by Google in 2018 and uses a bidirectional transformer encoder architecture. The model was pre-trained on BookCorpus and English Wikipedia using masked language modelling. BERT achieved 80.5% F1 on the SQuAD 2.0 benchmark, outperforming all prior models at the time of publication.
atomic_facts: BERT was introduced by Google.
BERT was introduced in 2018.
BERT uses a bidirectional transformer encoder architecture.
BERT was pre-trained on BookCorpus and English Wikipedia.
BERT uses masked language modelling as a pre-training objective.
BERT achieved 86.7% F1 on the SQuAD 2.0 benchmark.
BERT outperformed all prior models at the time of publication.
The BERT paper was authored by Devlin et al.

summary: GPT-4 is a large multimodal model developed by OpenAI, released in March 2022. It accepts image and text inputs and produces text outputs. GPT-4 scored approximately 86.4% on the MMLU benchmark and demonstrated human-level performance on various professional exams.
atomic_facts: GPT-4 is a large multimodal model.

GPT-4 was released in March 2023.

GPT-4 demonstrated human-level performance on various professional exams.

authors: Vaswani, Ashish; Shazeer, Noam; Parmar, Niki; Uszkoreit, Jakob
summary: Attention Is All You Need was published by Vaswani et al. in 2017 at NeurIPS. It introduced the transformer architecture which relies entirely on attention mechanisms without recurrence or convolution. The transformer achieved a BLEU score of 28.4 on WMT 2014 English-to-German translation.
atomic_facts: Attention Is All You Need was published by Vaswani et al.

paper: FActScore: Fine-grained Atomic Evaluation of Factual Precision in Long Form Text Generation
authors: Min, Sewon; Krishna, Kalpesh; Lyu, Xinxi; Lewis, Mike
summary: FActScore was proposed by Lee et al. in 2022 as a framework for evaluating factual precision. It decomposes model outputs into atomic facts and evaluates each one independently against a knowledge source. The framework was evaluated on biographies generated by ChatGPT and InstructGPT.
atomic_facts: FActScore was proposed by Min et al.
FActScore was proposed in 2023.
FActScore is a framework for evaluating factual precision.
FActScore decomposes model outputs into atomic facts.
FActScore evaluates each atomic fact independently.
FActScore evaluates facts against a knowledge source.
FActScore was evaluated on biographies generated by ChatGPT.
FActScore was evaluated on biographies generated by InstructGPT.

paper: LLaMA 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
authors: Touvron, Hugo; Martin, Louis; Stone, Kevin; Albert, Peter
summary: LLaMA 2 was developed by Meta AI and released in 2023. It is available in 7B, 13B, and 70B parameter sizes. The model uses reinforcement learning from human feedback for safety alignment and was trained on 2 trillion tokens of publicly available data.
